top_signals.title: Africa’s Richest Man Proposes To Build 700,000 Bpd Oil Refinery In Kenya
top_signals.summary: East Africa has spent decades exporting one commodity and importing another. The region holds roughly 4.7 billion barrels of crude oil reserves and more than 70 trillion cubic feet of natural gas across Uganda, South Sudan, Kenya and the DRC, according to the African Energy Commission (AFREC). Yet it imports 100% of its refined fuel after Kenya Petroleum Refineries Limited (KPRL), the region’s last operating refinery, shut down in 2013. Nigerian billionaire Aliko Dangote now says he intends to reverse that equation with a $17 billion (KSh2.2…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Canada in UK-Japan-Italy jet program as observer, paves way for others
Cabinet spokesman defends Takaichi’s grueling sleep schedule
top_signals.summary: The prime minister's social media post about being unable to get enough sleep drew criticism for running counter to workstyle reform efforts.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
